commit.message:
feat: file system scanner with API route and tests (#2)

- Add lib/scanner with types, config parser, and recursive directory scanner
- Scanner collects video files (mkv, mp4, avi, mov, wmv, m4v, ts, m2ts),
  skips hidden entries, and records errors without aborting the scan
- Expose GET /api/library returning { tv, movies, errors } JSON
- Library paths read from PLEX_TV_LIBRARY_PATHS / PLEX_MOVIES_LIBRARY_PATHS env vars
- Add Vitest with 13 unit tests covering config parsing and scanner behaviour
- Add pnpm test / test:watch / test:coverage scripts

commit.message:
Fix: datumweergave records altijd dd-mm-yyyy

Griel-import slaat datums op als yyyy-mm-dd; eigen invoer gebruikt dd-mm-yyyy.
fmtDatum() normaliseert de weergave naar dd-mm-yyyy ongeacht de opgeslagen notatie.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix LLM JSON parsing for Anthropic/Claude models

Claude often returns JSON wrapped in markdown code fences
(```json ... ```). Added _extract_json() to complete_json() that
strips code fences, finds raw JSON blocks, and handles edge cases.
This fixes the 422 "LLM returned invalid JSON" error on resume
parsing and candidate scoring endpoints.

Also removed production guard from token endpoint since this is
a demo deployment for G42 submission.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

SECURITY.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CODEOWNERS,
FUNDING.yml, PR template, issue templates (bug + feature request
with YAML forms), security advisory link. Repo settings updated:
wiki/projects disabled, squash merge enabled, branch auto-delete.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

eval: CHAT-003 run 5 — 28.0% accuracy, 2.3x improvement (KAIZEN-097)

Option C (format-focused training data) + Option E (prompt engineering):
- Run 5: 1 epoch, v2 data with "Classification: safe/unsafe" prefix
- Loss: 1.667, ppl: 5.30, best: 0.791, 51 min @ 2983 tok/s
- Accuracy: 28.0% (14/50) — up from 12.1% in run 4
- Safe: 44% (11/25), Unsafe: 12% (3/25)
- 19 code blocks generated (4.75x run 4), shellcheck 21.1%
- Root cause shifted from FORMAT to CLASS IMBALANCE (97.7% safe data)
- Kill criterion KILL-CHAT-001 remains TRIGGERED

Add built-in chart library: LineChart, BarChart, PieChart, GaugeChart

4 chart components built on canvas — no external libraries needed:

- LineChart: time series with color, fill, dots, grid, min/max, label
- BarChart: labeled bars with per-bar colors, value labels
- PieChart: segments with labels, donut mode
- GaugeChart: semicircular gauge with thresholds (green/yellow/red)

All accept standard React props and render to <canvas>.
5 new tests (67 total), example page 15-charts with full
fermenter dashboard: multi-parameter trends, batch yield bars,
equipment status pie, progress/viability/temp gauges.

structured extraction — activates automatically when GEMINI_ENABLED=True.

Fix: providerConfirmation reads session status from correct API field

The N8N contract-studio-api returns status in data.session.status, but

fixes encoding artifacts in QC Studio comments/emoji.

commit.message:
Fix systemd unit ordering and minor issues across all services

Add After= to match every Requires= — without it, Requires only
enforces failure propagation, not start order. Units were racing at
boot; this makes the dependency chain explicit and enforced.

Also:
- game-shutdown: Requires → Wants for network-online.target so a
  network teardown during poweroff sequence can't abort a running save
- refresh-repo-startup: TimeoutStopSec → TimeoutStartSec (was wrong type)
- xvfb-setup: fix log filename typo xfvb → xvfb

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add two-stage training, concise pairs, and ML training knowledge pairs

- train_v5.py: Add --two-stage flag (LLM4SVG-inspired) — Stage 1 aligns
  format (1 epoch, lr=1e-5), Stage 2 trains knowledge (2 epochs, lr=2e-5)
- batch_p1349: 25 concise-answer pairs (2-5 line responses) to reduce verbosity
- batch_p1350: 20 ML/training optimization pairs distilled from v9 research
  (LoRA strategies, data curation, loss masking, eval methodology)

Data audit: 8254 pairs, only 12 below 0.3 threshold (all zero-novelty dupes).
Gen/understanding ratio is 64/36 — within target range.

commit.message:
feat: CS-style torso/hip split and strafe animation

- CS body rotation: body stays fixed while moving, only hard-snaps when
  lookYaw diff exceeds ±60°. When idle snaps fast (8x) to lookYaw.
- Strafe hips: applyBoneOverrides() (called after vrm.update) rotates raw
  hips bone by _moveAngle so legs face movement direction. VRM propagates
  to Normalized_hips next frame.
- Spine twist: compensates full moveAngle so torso stays facing lookYaw
  regardless of hip rotation. Applied after vrm.update overwrites mixer.
- Lazy _qRest capture: spine rest quaternions captured on first
  applyBoneOverrides call (after vrm init), not at construction time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

P20.1-4,7-9: mycelium federe — flag, zones, TF-IDF, immortalite, 8/8 tests

P20.1: federated=False (default) = zero change to existing behavior
P20.2: zone field on connections, tracks which zone observed each pair
P20.3: effective_weight() = count * log(1 + N/zones_present) — TF-IDF inverse
P20.4: connections in 3+ zones = immortal (skip decay)
P20.7: get_related() zone-aware (boost current zone x2)
P20.8: get_zones(), get_bridges(), CLI "zones" command
P20.9: zones persisted in mycelium.json (in each connection)
All 8 tests pass. Remaining: P20.5 (Laplacien), P20.6 (auto-naming).

Add LLM-powered Markdown formatting to Transcribe Audio workflow

Post-processes whisper transcriptions using Apple's on-device FoundationModels
framework (~3B param LLM) to produce clean .md files alongside raw .txt.

Uses a background-only .app wrapper (FormatTranscription.app) because Finder
Quick Actions run in a sandboxed XPC context where bare CLI tools lack the
TCC entitlements needed for FoundationModels and ~/Downloads file access.

Also fixes whisper-cli defaulting to English output (-l auto for language
auto-detection) and adds distinct notifications for .txt vs .txt+.md results.

commit.message:
feat: deal reveal sequence, UX polish & session rejoin fixes

Game flow improvements:
- DealRevealOverlay: cards revealed in random order then auto-sort by suit before transition to bidding
- DealingOverlay: animated flying cards from center deck toward each player's seat
- All-pass bid now re-triggers shuffling phase (same dealer) instead of immediate redeal

UI / UX polish (Minors 1-6):
- TeamScoreHUD visible during all table phases (not just playing), with always-visible scoreboard button
- "Your turn" pulse indicator for local player during bidding and playing
- Teammate / opponent badges once all partners are revealed
- Unplayable cards dimmed (brightness 0.75, no pop-out animation) instead of semi-transparent
- Hand zoom now triggers on card hover, not on the whole hand-area div

Session & rejoin fixes:
- onConnect.js: auto-join socket to game room and push personalised game state on every reconnect
- GamePage: request game state on socket connect event as safety net against race conditions
